findings.title: Direct underlying donations can inflate the exchange rate until later minters receive zero shares

best_agent_match.title: Direct underlying donations can inflate the exchange rate until victim mints round to zero

findings.title: When total supply resets to zero, the next minter can capture stranded underlying at the initial exchange rate

best_agent_match.title: When total supply reaches zero, the next minter can capture any stranded underlying at the initial exchange rate

findings.title: Liquidation treats any zero-decimal collateral market as NFT collateral

best_agent_match.title: Liquidation misclassifies any zero-decimal collateral market as an NFT market

best_agent_match.title: Any account can sweep arbitrary non-underlying tokens out of the market to the admin

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation for ERC721 Collateral Uses Inconsistent Calculations Leading to Undercollateralization
rejected_candidates.reason: The code repays `possibleRepayAmount` and then computes seized collateral from `actualRepayAmount`; that is internally consistent from the available code. The supported issue here is the `decimals()==0` type check, not a demonstrated under-seizure bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Implementation Contract Not Validated Before Delegation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-only upgradeability trust assumption inherent to the proxy pattern. No permissionless exploit or unexpected privilege bypass is shown.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Check for Zero Initial Exchange Rate
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract already requires `initialExchangeRateMantissa > 0`. Complaining about the absence of an upper bound is an admin-controlled deployment parameter, not a standalone protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Reserve Factor Can Be Set to 100%
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an explicit governance-controlled parameter bounded by `reserveFactorMaxMantissa`; it is not a hidden vulnerability in the implementation.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in exchangeRateStoredInternal if Total Supply is Zero
rejected_candidates.reason: `exchangeRateStoredInternal()` explicitly returns `initialExchangeRateMantissa` when `totalSupply == 0`, so the claimed division-by-zero path does not exist.

rejected_candidates.title: Protocol Seize Share is Hardcoded Without Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a design choice, not a security issue causing realistic protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No Maximum Borrow Rate Validation in Interest Rate Model
rejected_candidates.reason: `accrueInterest()` already reverts if `borrowRateMantissa > borrowRateMaxMantissa`. Installing a malicious model is an admin action, and the runtime check prevents silent over-accrual.

rejected_candidates.title: ERC20 Transfer Handling May Fail Silently for Certain Tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `doTransferOut()` inspects returndata and reverts on failure; it does not silently succeed when the token returns false or behaves non-compliantly.

rejected_candidates.title: Delegatecall Risk in OErc20Delegator Allows Implementation to Modify Delegator Storage
rejected_candidates.reason: That is the intended behavior of a delegatecall proxy and does not identify a concrete vulnerability beyond the normal trust model of upgradeable contracts.
